title: Async Channels and Message Passing
content:
Channels let async tasks communicate by passing messages. Tokio provides several channel types for different use cases.

mpsc (multi-producer, single-consumer): The most common channel. Multiple senders, one receiver. Bounded (backpressure) or unbounded.

oneshot: Send a single value. Perfect for request-response patterns where you spawn a task and wait for one result.

broadcast: Multi-producer, multi-consumer. Every receiver gets every message. Good for event broadcasting.

watch: Single-producer, multi-consumer for a single value that changes over time. Receivers always see the latest value.

Channels are the preferred way to communicate between async tasks -- they avoid shared mutable state and the complexity of locks.
keyPoints: mpsc is the workhorse channel: multiple senders, one receiver, with backpressure
oneshot sends exactly one value -- perfect for spawning a task and getting a reply
broadcast delivers every message to every subscriber
watch lets receivers always see the latest value
Bounded channels provide backpressure -- senders block when the buffer is full
Channels avoid shared mutable state and are the preferred communication mechanism
